course_description:
This course involves 400-480 hours of industry experience, through a work integrated learning (WIL) placement in a production or service enterprise. It provides students with the opportunity to apply the theoretical knowledge and research skills they have developed earlier in the Master of Food Science and Technology program in a real-world industry setting.

Through this immersive experience, students will gain a deeper understanding of industry operations, expectations, and professional standards, enhancing their career readiness and employability.

Please note the following important conditions:

Information about enrolling in a placement course, including registering your interest and application deadlines, can be found here: science.uq.edu.au/placements . Eligible students will apply for UQ-sourced placement opportunities in a competitive selection process.

This course offers students practical, industry-based experience and the opportunity to strengthen professional skills in a real-world setting. It bridges theory and practice by allowing students to apply their theoretical concepts and knowledge, while understanding industry operations before graduation.

Students will complete 400-480 hours of placementﾠin an enterprise such as a food production company or a food certification or food safety authority (e.g. Safe Food Queensland). Placements are coordinated by Science Industry Placements. Students should note that UQ Industry Partners decide which applicant(s) are offered a placements, and this is a highly competitive process. Once a student accepts a placement arranged with an industry host partner, they cannot withdraw from the offer and drop their course enrolment , except under extenuating circumstances, which must be with the Course Coordinator on a case-by-case basis.

Students must attend compulsory orientation and employability workshops before and after the placement. The sessions focus on the importance of self-reflection, professional growth and effectively communicating acquired skills and experiences to potential employers.

Each host organisation will appoint a Placement Supervisor. The Course Coordinator acts as the Academic Supervisor, addressing any issues arising during the placement, and monitoring progress with both the student and the Placement Supervisor.

Placements are generally unpaid. UQ insurance coverage applies to unpaid placements. If the host organisation casually employs the student during their placement, they must provide insurance coverage.

During the placement, students will adhere to the UQ Student Charter (https://ppl.app.uq.edu.au/content/3.60.01-student-charter) and the UQ Placements in Coursework Programs Policy (https://ppl.app.uq.edu.au/content/3.10.04-placements-coursework-programs), as well as act in accordance with host organisation’s policies, procedures and regulations. Students may be asked to enter into a confidentiality and intellectual property agreement with the host organisation. Students should seek their own independent legal advice to ensure they are aware of their obligations and before signing such agreements. The UQ Union (https://www.uqu.com.au/student-support/legal) offers a free legal service to all UQ Students who are currently enrolled.

For course-related administrative queries please email agriculture@enquire.uq.edu.au (http://enquire.uq.edu.au) (mailto:agriculture@enquire.uq.edu.au) or phone 3365 1171.

Course Changes in Response to Previous Student Feedback: ePortfolio submissions have been replaced with assessment submissions links in the Assessment Folder on the FOOD7021 Learn.UQ Ultra site and InPlace, to make the process simpler and more user-friendly.

At the School of Agriculture and Food Sustainability, we are committed to creating an inclusive and empowering learning environment for all students. We value and respect the diverse range of experiences our students bring to their education, and we believe that this diversity is crucial for fostering a rich culture of knowledge-sharing and meaningful exploration. We hold students and staff accountable for actively contributing to establishing a respectful and supportive learning environment.

Bullying, harassment, and discrimination in any form are strictly against our principles and against UQ Policy, and will not be tolerated. If you have any concerns about your experience in this course, we encourage you to tell a member of the course teaching team. Your well-being and a positive learning atmosphere are of utmost importance to us.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:
restrictions: Permission required to enrol. See course description for further information.

course_contacts.name: Dr Deirdre Mikkelsen
course_contacts.notes: Consultation is by appointment. To make an appointment, please email.

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
The compulsory Orientation Workshopﾠwill be held on the first day of Semester. The compulsory post-placement Employability Workshopﾠwillﾠtake place during Teaching Week 13 (i.e. Week 14 of the FOOD7021 Schedule). Refer to the UQ Public Timetable for details.
Individual, face-to-face Mock Interviews will be scheduled during the first week of the Semester 1 exam period. Date and venue will be confirmed on the course Ultra site.
To assist students with the placement allocation process, optional workshops areﾠheld in the semester prior to enrolment. Workshop details are advertised onﾠ
StudentHub
ﾠunder 'Events'.
If you have any concerns regarding your official timetable or course allocations, or there are no suitable class times available, contact Faculty of Science Timetable
science.mytimetable@uq.edu.au
for advice.
If you have any questions about missing class activities, please contact your course coordinator.
course_aims: Toﾠ enable students to apply their theoretical knowledge and skills to a food related workplace situation, and to acquire more detailed knowledge and professional skills to enhance employability.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate professional skills in the workplace including effective oral and written communication, team interaction skills, time management skills, ability to work independently and uphold ethical practice.
Link theory and practice by demonstrating practical processing, research, laboratory, or problem-solving skills in a professional work environment.
Evaluate management, production, and laboratory systems in a professional work environment.
Critically reflect on events and activities during the placement using the SEAL template.

assessment_details.title: Orientation Workshop Quiz (In-class, Online)
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Work integrated learning, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: At the conclusion of the full-day compulsory workshop, which prepares students for their 13-week industry placement with food industry hosts across South-East Queensland, students attempt a timed online quiz. The quiz will be based on the workshop content including essential information, resources and expectations to ensure a successful and professional placement experience. It is anticipated that this assessment will facilitate students' understanding of workplace culture, professional communication and employer expectations in the Australian food industry. Additionally, students will develop awareness of workplace health and safety requirements. They will also have learnt strategies for adapting to new work environments and building professional relationships. Duration: 20 minutes Marks: 20 marks Attempts: One attempt only Question types: Multiple choice, true/false, short answer scenario-based questions. Further details are available on the FOOD7021 Learn.UQ Ultra site. This test will be taken on a UQ campus location with secure internet service. Please bring your own device for this Quiz and ensure that it is fully charged . Loan laptops may be available from the UQ Library. Troubleshooting – if students experience any technical difficulties during the online test/quiz: Do not close the browser window. Seek assistance from the Course Coordinator during this in-class assessment first. Contact AskUs support.my.uq.edu.au/app/chat/chat_launch_lib – if the technical issue cannot be resolved, ask for a copy of the chat. Document the problem – if you receive an error message or similar, take a copy of the message, note the time/date of the problem, which web browser you used, exactly what you were doing at the time and any other relevant information. Email your course coordinator (copy in AGFS Admin – agriculture@enquire.uq.edu.au ) with your documented evidence and a copy of the AskUs chat. Any delays in this communication being received may impact the advice that could be provided to you. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The online assessment is completed in the FOOD7021 Learn.UQ Ultra site under the appropriate assessment title.
Students will submit this task during class time, under invigilation conditions.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Extensions or deferrals are not available for the Orientation Workshop Quiz as this assessment occurs prior to the placement commencing. This assessment is important in the placement preparation timeline and must be completed as scheduled to confirm student eligibility and readiness for industry placement.
If extenuating circumstances prevent a student from attending the Orientation Workshop and completing the quiz in person, and the student provides appropriate supporting documentation, the Course Coordinator will assess the situation on a case‑by‑case basis to determine the student’s suitability to proceed to placement and, if appropriate, will work with the Science Industry Placement team to liaise with the host supervisor about adjusting the placement start date.
Late submission
You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .
This assessment is completed in a time-limited class environment and is required for students commencing their placements on time. Students cannot complete this task outside of the Orientation Workshop
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .

assessment_details.title: Learning Contract & Risk Assessment
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Learning Contract & Risk Assessment Document: Students must negotiate the Learning Contract and Risk Assessment with their Placement Supervisor during the first week of placement. When addressing the Learning Contract, discussion must include confidentiality requirements . The completion of the Risk Assessment is done to identify hazards associated with placement activities. This is a critical task , as the document serves as evidence for any workplace insurance claims. This assessment must be completed in person. The relevant template is available in the Assessment Folder on the FOOD7021 Learn.UQ Ultra site. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. The use of AI or MT must be acknowledged appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit via the submission link in the Assessment Folder on the FOOD7021 Learn.UQ Ultra site.
You should also retain an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit.
Legal Declaration:
By submitting your work via this website, you formally declare that (1) it is your own original work, and no part of the work has been copied from any other source or person except where due acknowledgement is made; (2) no part of the work has been previously submitted for assessment in this or any other institution; and (3) you have read the Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and understand its implications.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Submission of an Extension of Assessment Due Date (EADD) should be completed online through your My Requests tab via my.UQ Dashboard , in accordance with the University's Assessment Procedure (located in the Policies and Procedures section of this course profile) which outlines the requirements for requesting extensions to assessment due dates. Extensions require the correct evidence/documentation to support the requested length of time of the extension.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
For example, a report worth a maximum of 40 marks, submitted 28 hours late will attract a penalty of 8 marks, calculated as 2 periods of 24 hours x 10% x 40 marks.
Assessment deadlines are firm and must be met. Late submission without permission or non-submission of assessable work will result in penalties being applied . Late submission of assessment will only be granted for documented medical reasons, accident, bereavement, jury service and other circumstances allowed in the UQ Policies and Procedures Library (PPL).

assessment_details.title: Sequential Reflective Practices
assessment_details.due_date: Reflective Practice 1 due 17:00 12/03/2026 Reflective Practice 2 due 17:00 26/03/2026 Reflective Practice 3 due 17:00 9/04/2026 Reflective Practice 4 due 17:00 23/04/2026 Reflective Practice 5 due 17:00 7/05/2026 Reflective Practice 6 due 17:00 29/05/2026
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Using the SEAL template provided, students will reflect on events and activities during their placement that align to the following topics: Reflective Practice 1: Challenges in the Workplace . Reflective Practice 2: Communication . Reflective Practice 3: Select 1 topic ONLY to reflect on from: Work Tasks OR Teamwork . Reflective Practice 4: Select 1 topic ONLY to reflect on from: Working with New Technologies & AI in the Food Industry OR Adaptability & Flexibility . Reflective Practice 5: Food Industry insights . Reflective Practice 6: Lifelong learning . Each reflection will consist of two components: Written : Describe the situation, its impact and the actions taken. Recorded : Summarise key learnings in a 2-minute video. Reflections must be submitted using the appropriate links in the Assessment Folder on the FOOD7021 Learn.UQ Ultra site. Submissions are due by 17:00 Thursday in Weeks 3, 5, 7, 9 and 11, and by 17:00 Friday in Week 14 (after the Employability Workshop). These due dates apply throughout the placement schedule, including the mid-semester break. Each reflection is worth 7% of your overall grade. Further details are available on the FOOD7021 Learn.UQ Ultra site. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. The use of AI or MT must be referenced appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Sequential Self-Evaluation & Feedback Action Plan for Supervisor Report 1
assessment_details.weight: 7% Self-Evaluation contributes 3% and Feedback Action Plan contributes 4% to the overall weighting of 7%
assessment_details.due_date: Self-Evaluation 16/03/2026 9:00 am Feedback Action Plan 25/03/2026 5:00 pm
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Self-Evaluation Purpose: The purpose of this self‑evaluation is to encourage students to critically reflect on their early performance, professional conduct and progress in the first three weeks of their industry placement. By assessing their strengths, areas for development and alignment with workplace expectations, students build self‑awareness and take an active role in their professional growth. This reflection also supports the interpretation of Supervisor Report 1, enabling students to better understand supervisor feedback and prepare for productive discussions that strengthen their workplace skills and contribute to a successful placement experience. Student Responsibilities: By Monday of the week the Supervisor Report 1 is due, the student must: Export and compile logbook and attendance Combine relevant weekly timesheets from the Excel Logbook into a single PDF. Submit the following via the appropriate links in InPlace: Compiled logbook and attendance pdf showing workplace attendance and a summary of weekly activities. Completed self-evaluation covering the first three weeks of placement. Weighting: 3% of final grade Feeddback Action Plan Purpose: As part of your professional development you will analyse the feedback provided in your Supervisor Report 1 and develop a Feedback Action Plan and submit it via InPlace. This plan will help you to reflect on your strengths, identify areas for improvement, and outline concrete steps to enhance your employability and professional skills. By completing this task, you will: Critically analyse feedback from your industry host supervisor. Identify key strengths and areas for growth. Develop a structured plan for improving technical, professional, and communication skills. Align your personal development with industry expectations and career goals. Weighting: 4% of final grade Further information can be found in the FOOD7021 Learn.UQ Ultra site. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. The use of AI or MT must be referenced appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Student to submit pdf of relevant timesheets and self-reflection via InPlace.
Legal Declaration:
By submitting your work via this website, you formally declare that (1) it is your own original work, and no part of the work has been copied from any other source or person except where due acknowledgement is made; (2) no part of the work has been previously submitted for assessment in this or any other institution; and (3) you have read the Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and understand its implications.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Extensions or deferrals are not available for the Self-Reflection and Feedback Action Plan for Supervisor Report 1. This assessment occurs at a critical early point in the placement and must be completed as scheduled to support early progress monitoring, confirm student eligibility for industry placement and compliance with UQ census date deadlines.
Late submission
You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .
This assessment will incur a 100% late penalty, as it must be completed on time to maintain the placement schedule and feedback process.

assessment_details.title: Supervisor Report 1
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Purpose: The Placement Supervisor will assess the student’s progress, performance and conduct approximately three weeks after placement begins . This feedback helps identify development opportunities for the remainder of the placement. Industry Placement Supervisor Responsibilities: The assessment request is automatically sent to the industry placement supervisor, granting access to complete the Supervisor Report 1 in InPlace. The placement supervisor is encouraged to use the report as an opportunity to conduct a formal performance review session with the student and provide face-to-face feedback before submitting the report electronically. Further information can be found in the FOOD7021 Learn.UQ Ultra site. The Supervisor Report must be submitted by the due date by the industry placement supervisor or their authorised representative. Students who achieve 'Unsatisfactory' in any of the five evaluated areas, OR receives an overall mark of 50% or less, will have their placement paused. They will be referred to the Course Coordinator and required to attend a feedback and academic progression outcomes meeting. The student will need to implement a one-week performance plan. The Placement Supervisor will be asked to report again at the week's end, and if performance is still unsatisfactory, the student's placement will be terminated and they will have to withdraw from the course. Further information can be found in the FOOD7021 Learn.UQ Ultra site. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. The use of AI or MT must be referenced appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Placement Supervisor to complete report in InPlace.
In accordance with the Faculty of Science, School of Agriculture and Food Sustainability – Food Science & Technology Industry Placements Information for Supervisors Handbook, the Industry Host Supervisor Report must be submitted by the due date by the host supervisor or their authorised representative. Timely submission of this report is essential, as delays may affect a student’s stipend payments (where applicable) and may also limit a student’s ability to withdraw from the course prior to the UQ census date without incurring financial or academic penalties.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Extensions or deferrals are not available for Supervisor Report 1. This assessment provides an essential early evaluation of the student’s placement performance and must be submitted as scheduled to support timely progress monitoring, confirm student safety and suitability for continued placement, and ensure students can meet critical UQ census date requirements.
Late submission
As these assessments are completed by the industry host supervisor and not generated by the student, students are not responsible for late submissions for the Sequence Supervisor Reports.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.late_submission: As these assessments are completed by the industry host supervisor and not generated by the student, students are not responsible for late submissions for the Sequence Supervisor Reports.
assessment_details.ai_statement:
Purpose: The Placement Supervisor will assess the student’s progress, performance and conduct approximately three weeks after placement begins . This feedback helps identify development opportunities for the remainder of the placement. Industry Placement Supervisor Responsibilities: The assessment request is automatically sent to the industry placement supervisor, granting access to complete the Supervisor Report 1 in InPlace. The placement supervisor is encouraged to use the report as an opportunity to conduct a formal performance review session with the student and provide face-to-face feedback before submitting the report electronically. Further information can be found in the FOOD7021 Learn.UQ Ultra site. The Supervisor Report must be submitted by the due date by the industry placement supervisor or their authorised representative. Students who achieve 'Unsatisfactory' in any of the five evaluated areas, OR receives an overall mark of 50% or less, will have their placement paused. They will be referred to the Course Coordinator and required to attend a feedback and academic progression outcomes meeting. The student will need to implement a one-week performance plan. The Placement Supervisor will be asked to report again at the week's end, and if performance is still unsatisfactory, the student's placement will be terminated and they will have to withdraw from the course. Further information can be found in the FOOD7021 Learn.UQ Ultra site. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. The use of AI or MT must be referenced appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Progress Reflection for Supervisor Report 2
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Purpose: The purpose of this progress reflection is to support students in critically evaluating their development, performance and professional growth over the duration of their industry placement. By reviewing their achievements, challenges and their progress since receiving feedback provided in Supervisor Report 1, students consolidate their learning and assess how effectively they have met workplace expectations. Student Responsibilities: By Monday of the week the Supervisor Report 2 is due, the student must: Export and compile logbook and attendance Combine relevant weekly timesheets from the Excel Logbook into a single PDF. Submit the following via the appropriate links in InPlace: Compiled logbook and attendance pdf showing workplace attendance and a summary of weekly activities. Completed progress reflection form. Further information can be found in the FOOD7021 Learn.UQ Ultra site. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. The use of AI or MT must be referenced appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Student to submit pdf of relevant timesheets and progress reflection via the appropriate links in InPlace.
Legal Declaration:
By submitting your work via this website, you formally declare that (1) it is your own original work, and no part of the work has been copied from any other source or person except where due acknowledgement is made; (2) no part of the work has been previously submitted for assessment in this or any other institution; and (3) you have read the Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and understand its implications.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Students with acceptable reasons (medical or compassionate reasons or as a result of exceptional circumstances) may apply for an extension of the deadline.
Submission of an Extension of Assessment Due Date (EADD) should be completed online through your My Requests tab via my.UQ Dashboard before the due date , in accordance with the University's Assessment Procedure (located in the Policies and Procedures section of this course profile) which outlines the requirements for requesting extensions to assessment due dates. Extensions require the correct evidence/documentation to support the requested length of time of the extension.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
For example, a report worth a maximum of 40 marks, submitted 28 hours late will attract a penalty of 8 marks, calculated as 2 periods of 24 hours x 10% x 40 marks.
Assessment deadlines are firm and must be met. Late submission without permission or non-submission of assessable work will result in penalties being applied . Late submission of assessment will only be granted for documented medical reasons, accident, bereavement, jury service and other circumstances allowed in the UQ Policies and Procedures Library (PPL).

assessment_details.title: Employability Workshop Elevator Pitch
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: As part of the compulsory employability workshop, each student will deliver a 60-90 seconds elevator pitch to the class that effectively communicates their professional identity, industry placement experience, and career aspirations. This exercise will help students refine their ability to present themselves confidently and concisely to potential employers, industry professionals and networking contacts. By completing this task, students will: Develop a clear and compelling professional introduction. Effectively highlight key skills, industry experience, and career goals. Practice confident and engaging verbal communication. Participate in productive peer feedback to refine their pitch for real-world networking situations, as part of our FOOD7021 community of learning. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. The use of AI or MT must be referenced appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Exam Week 1 - One-on-one Mock Interview
assessment_details.due_date: 8/06/2026 - 12/06/2026 The mock interview will take place in Exam Week 1, with the exact date and times TBC via an announcement on the FOOD7021 Learn.UQ Ultra site.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: As part of a " Mock Interview" , the course coordinator will ask specific questions related to any of the six reflection topics submitted by the student during the semester. Overall, the interview will last 4-5 minutes. Students will be made aware of the timer, which will sound at 4:30 minutes, allowing them to conclude their answers. One follow-up question will be asked - answers provided could only improve the grade. The mock interview will be recorded to ensure accuracy in grading and for review purposes. Further information can be found in the FOOD7021 Learn.UQ blackboard site. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 12
learning_activities.topic: Professional Experience Students undertake 400-480 hours of workplace experience in a host organisation under the supervision of the Placement Supervisor. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

Orientation Workshop This activity will take place in-person on the first day of semester. Attendance is compulsory. The orientation session will be specific to FOOD7021, covering professionalism, the course requirements and discussing assessment expectations and submission requirements. Learning outcomes: L01

Employability Workshop The post-placement Employability Workshop is held during Teaching Week 13 of semester. Attendance is compulsory. This will be an in-person activity, where students will have the opportunity to unpack their recent work integrated learning experience, as well as have the opportunity to network and hear from previous FOOD7021 graduates currently working in the Food Manufacturing Industry in South-East Queensland. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . You'll also need to be aware of the following policies and procedures while completing this course: Work Integrated Learning and Work Experience Policy Learning activities
